news.title: Oil hits one-month high as Mideast war keeps investors on edge
news.description: Brent crude hit on Monday its highest price since June due to renewed fighting between the United States and Iran, while Asian equities were mixed as investors weighed the fallout of a prolonged Middle East war.Brent rose above $91 a barrel, its highest price since June 11.

news.title: Dow Jones Futures: Oil Prices Rise Amid Iran News; Google, Tesla Ahead
news.description: Dow Jones futures: Iran attacks killed three U.S. servicemembers. Google earnings and capex guidance will be key this week. Tesla, Intel, GE Vernova also report.

news.title: Nasdaq, Dow, S&P 500 Futures Edge Higher Ahead Of Key Earnings Week Even As Middle East Tensions Continue: DJT, NVDA, SLS, PANW Stocks In Focus
news.description: Alphabet, Tesla, and Intel are among the key companies reporting their second-quarter results later this week.

news.title: Markets brace for return to all-out war in Iran amid rising U.S. death toll, while former NATO commander warns Suez Canal could be next
news.description: Retired Adm. James Stavridis told CNN on Sunday that Trump now has three options, and "and none of them are good."

news.title: Oil Price Tops $90 as Iran War Escalates: What It Means for Crypto
news.description: Brent crude tops $90 as the Iran war chokes Hormuz flows, lifting Fed hike bets and pressuring Bitcoin.

news.title: Cathie Wood sells $11.7 million of tumbling semiconductor stock
news.description: Cathie Wood, head of Ark Investment Management, has built a reputation for backing artificial intelligence stocks. But even her highest-conviction holdings aren't immune to profit-taking. That's exactly what she's doing with AMD this week, selling more shares after the chipmaker's recent pullback. .

news.description: Chip and AI-linked stocks have whacked in recent weeks after a sharp run-up, while inflation concerns, the U.S.-Iran conflict, and stretched valuations have also weighed on sentiment.
